mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% as of 2017-01-15 indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment gains
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals strengthening consumer demand and housing activity that precedes broad-based economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 390,422 million and HOUST at 629 thousand as of 2017-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of co-acceleration events, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Interaction between rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL growth signals monetary tightening in response to inflation, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises 0.25% or more AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in the quarter following the data release.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,270.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2017-01-15 (Q4 2016 estimate)
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Interaction between rising INDPRO and declining PAYEMS signals stagflationary pressure where supply constraints drive production gains while labor market weakens, creating earnings uncertainty that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines or grows less than 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.07 and PAYEMS at 131,190 thousand as of 2017-01-15
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.
